methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $2M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Duranium is a Pre-Seed stage climate and energy company founded by Berkley Noble that has raised $2M+. The climate and energy sector includes a broader peer universe of 21 private companies and 1 public comparable (LNZA), with notable private peers including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape. However, the peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within Duranium's valuation tier to support a reliable median-based approach.

The stage-progression method is applicable and generates the primary valuation signal. Using the $2M total raised as a baseline proxy and applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from Pre-Seed to the subsequent funding round yields an implied valuation of $5M. This method reflects the expected markup associated with early-stage capital rounds and execution progress.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $4M to $6M, with a median of $5M. This estimate rests on a single method given the sparsity of near-peer data points. Key uncertainties include (1) the absence of same-stage climate and energy comparables to validate the 2.5x multiplier against sector-specific norms, and (2) the limited operational history available to assess de-risking. Any refinement of this range would benefit from additional Pre-Seed or Seed financings in the climate tech subsector.
